findings.message: Callout assigns Pericles.3's workload-migration behavior to Pericles.2 (Gen 2). §2.2 describes Pericles.2 as the 9HP analog FE respin family with mission-tuned variants — no DSP/workload migration. §2.3 describes the 12LP+ bridge die (Pericles.3) absorbing stable DSP blocks. The migration belongs to Gen 3, not Gen 2.
findings.suggested_fix: Either rewrite the callout bullet to say 'Gen 3: workloads migrate into 12LP+' (matching §2.3), or rewrite §2.2/§2.3 to put workload migration in Gen 2 (matching the callout). The detail-side framing is the load-bearing one — recommend correcting the callout.
findings.load_bearing_justification: The callout is the page-1 reader-anchor; the Gen-1/Gen-2/Gen-3 generation taxonomy IS the strategic thesis. A reader who stops after the callout has the wrong mental model of the platform. Critical.

findings.claim_excerpt: the FPGA is the measurement instrument
findings.message: Callout asserts the FPGA's role as 'measurement instrument that tells us which compute should move into the 12LP+ chiplet ASIC' — no detailed section elaborates on the measurement methodology or what 'tells us' means operationally. §2.1 mentions the FPGA-as-measurement-instrument framing but does not elaborate the methodology. Reader has no way to evaluate the operational claim.
findings.suggested_fix: Either add a §2.x subsection elaborating the FPGA-as-measurement-instrument methodology (what telemetry is captured, what 'tells us' means operationally, how the decision to migrate a compute block is made), or soften the callout to remove the operational claim (e.g., 'Gen 1 platform' without the instrument framing).
